header.note: Data-driven immutable rails. Any change here REQUIRES a new seal (ADR + Forge audit card + Chronicle receipt). See CLAUDE.md > Change-Control Law.

kinematics.turn_law: 3-DOF point mass (B2, v1.6r0): psi_dot = (g0/V)*(n*sin(phi)/cos(gamma)); gamma_dot = (g0/V)*(n*cos(phi)-cos(gamma)); reduces to psi_dot = g0*tan(phi)/V for the level coordinated turn (gamma=0, n=1/cos(phi)). The no-arg straight golden keeps the pure great-circle kinematic tail.
kinematics.flight_model: B3 limits & stall: tas integrated from thrust-drag (B1); flight-path angle gamma is a stored state driven by commanded load factor n (B2); altitude earned (alt_dot=V*sin gamma). Constant density rho0=1.225. B3 (v1.7r0): n is bounded per-airframe by the structural g limits (n_min_struct/n_max_struct) AND the C_Lmax aerodynamic ceiling n_aero=cl_max*qS/(m*g0); below the corner speed n_aero binds and the turn collapses (accelerated stall). Retires the B2 global [-3,9] placeholder. No new det_math; post-stall lift held at C_Lmax (no departure / ISA density = later phases).

determinism.flags: NO_LIBM NO_FAST_MATH NO_FMA NO_VINCENTY NO_KEPLER NO_X87

kin.note: Auxiliary NON-geographic block for client-side prediction (netcode layer 4b). Carries per-aircraft bank (phi), true airspeed (tas), and — since KIN-002 (seal v1.6r0, snapshot protocol 3) — flight-path angle (gamma), so a remote/late-join can re-seed a kernel from the full 7-tuple state (lat,lon,psi,phi,alt,tas,gamma). Same ZigZag+LEB128 encoding as GEO-001; transmitted as a SEPARATE snapshot section so GEO-001 stays geography-only. Lossy by quantization — never fed back as canonical (see CLAUDE.md determinism rules).

weapon.note: Auxiliary NON-geographic block for multiplayer GUNNERY replication (first sealed v1.12r0 at snapshot protocol 3 -> 4; the G4 magazine `ammo` joined it at v1.14r0, protocol 4 -> 5). A third self-delimiting snapshot section, present iff protocol >= 4, carrying the Step 7 weapon state so a remote/late-join client can DRAW the dogfight (tracer rounds, HP bars, kills, AND a rounds-remaining counter) instead of reading it out-of-band from the local kernel: (a) per-aircraft hp + fire_cd (the 8th/9th canonical f64) and — for protocol >= 5 — ammo (the 10th canonical f64), then (b) a projectile count and, per live ballistic round, a GeoPoint (bearing == round heading psi), its carried damage, and its integer ttl + owner. Same ZigZag+LEB128 encoding as GEO-001; hp/fire_cd/damage are quantized (1e3), ammo is quantized at unit scale (1e0 — a pure integer counter, so exact AND compact, like ttl/owner), while ttl/owner are integer counters carried EXACTLY (not quantized). Transmitted as a SEPARATE section so GEO-001 stays geography-only and KIN-002 framing is byte-identical. Lossy by quantization for the f64 fields -> never fed back as canonical (the world_hash source of truth is Kernel::snapshot(); this wire is downstream presentation/transport only, see CLAUDE.md determinism rules). No kernel/golden change: hp/fire_cd/ammo/damage already live in the canonical state, so all 10 goldens are byte-identical across this (and the v1.12r0) wire reseal.

weapons.model: G1 ballistic projectiles (Step 7 guns, v1.9r0): a fired round is a deterministic point mass on the same sphere — the n=0 (no lift) / thrust=0 specialization of the aircraft 3-DOF step. Gravity acts along the flight path and a lumped quadratic drag bleeds speed; the heading psi is fixed (the closed-form great-circle step carries the geodesic); each round has an integer time-to-live and despawns on ttl OR on hitting the ground. NO new det_math. Rounds are CANONICAL kernel state hashed into the world_hash (a projectile block appended to the snapshot — that block, n=0 for gun-less scenarios, is why the 6 prior scenario goldens + Sphere moved at v1.9r0). G1 defaults are GLOBAL constants in the kernel (muzzle 850 m/s added to firer TAS, drag k=2e-4, ttl=250 ticks) — like RHO0/V_MIN they live in code, not as rails. NOT yet on the GEO-001/KIN wire (deferred, like phi/tas were pre-4b). Hit detection + damage are G2; per-airframe weapon roster + fire-rate are G3.
weapons.damage: G2 hit detection + per-aircraft hitpoints (v1.10r0): a round within HIT_RADIUS (60 m, horizontal great-circle via the spherical law of cosines vs cos(HIT_RADIUS/R) — acos is monotone so NO det_acos) AND HIT_ALT_GATE (60 m vertical) of an ALIVE enemy (not the firer) deals DAMAGE_PER_ROUND (25) and despawns. Each aircraft carries hp (start 100); hp<=0 == DEAD -> its flight integration is skipped (it freezes), it can no longer fire, and it is excluded from hit tests (rounds pass through the corpse). hp is the 8th per-aircraft snapshot f64 (appended after gamma) -> all 8 prior goldens moved at v1.10r0 (hp=100 identical). Per-tick point test (swept/continuous collision deferred); per-airframe HP/armament are G3. Still NO new det_math (det_sin/det_cos only).
weapons.roster: G3 per-airframe weapon roster + fire-rate (v1.11r0): the G1/G2 global gun constants move into each envelope. New envelope scalars (tuning.* in data/tuning/envelopes/, loaded via envelopes.AERO_FIELDS): hp_start (airframe toughness), muzzle_v_mps (added to firer TAS), damage_per_round (CARRIED by each round so lethality is fixed at fire time), rof_interval_ticks (min ticks between shots). Firing is gated by a per-aircraft fire-rate cooldown (fire_cd, the 9th per-aircraft snapshot f64; decrement-then-fire => shots exactly rof_interval ticks apart). The round carries its damage (the 7th projectile f64). So airframes differ: .50cal platforms (P-47D/P-51) = high rate, durable; 20mm cannons (A6M2/La-7) = high damage/round; A6M2 = glass cannon (hard hit, slow, fragile). Drag/ttl stay global; START_HP stays the no-arg/Sphere default. STILL no new det_math, no wire change. Initial balance pass (relative WWII character; retuning stays data-only). Values: P-47D hp150/850/12/3t, A6M2 hp70/650/40/9t, etc.
weapons.ammunition: G4 finite ammunition (v1.13r0): each envelope carries ammo_start (the per-airframe magazine, abstract rounds; loaded via envelopes.AERO_FIELDS). Firing is now gated on ammo > 0 as well as the fire-rate cooldown; one round is consumed per shot (ammo decremented, clamped at 0). An empty magazine falls silent ('Winchester') — the trigger may stay held and the aircraft alive, but no further rounds spawn. ammo is the 10th per-aircraft snapshot f64 (appended after fire_cd) -> all 9 prior goldens moved at v1.13r0 (ammo constant/identical for the unchanged scenarios), plus the NEW GOLDEN-SK-Winchester-001 (an A6M2 empties its 100-round magazine at tick 891 and its cannon goes silent). Relative WWII endurance: A6M2 fewest (100, the famous ~60-rpg 20mm cannon), P-47D most (340, eight deep .50-cal belts). STILL no new det_math (a pure integer-valued counter, like fire_cd). ammo lives in the CANONICAL state and — since v1.14r0 — also rides the WEAPON-001 snapshot wire (the 10th per-aircraft field, unit-scale quantized, snapshot protocol 4 -> 5), so a remote/late-join client shows a rounds-remaining counter (exactly as fire_cd became kernel-state at G3 then rode the wire at v1.12r0). Transport-only reseal: ammo already lived in the canonical state, so all 10 goldens stay byte-identical. See ADR-Step7-Guns-G4 (counter) and ADR-Step7-Guns-WireTransport-Ammo-v1.14r0 (wire).
weapons.convergence: Gun convergence / harmonization (Step 7 guns, v1.15r0): each envelope carries convergence_m (per-airframe boresight range, loaded via envelopes.AERO_FIELDS). SEADS models a single CENTERLINE battery, so lateral convergence is undefined; harmonization is realized as VERTICAL boresight zeroing (gravity-drop compensation). A fired round's initial gamma is offset UP by the flat-fire drop-compensation angle delta = 0.5*g0*convergence_m / v^2 (v = firer TAS + muzzle_v_mps) so its ballistic trajectory crosses the aim (sight) line at convergence_m instead of always falling below the nose. Pure +-*/ => NO new det_math. This is a KERNEL spawn-geometry change (round initial gamma), so it moves the 3 firing goldens (Gunfire/Hit/Winchester); the 7 non-firing goldens are byte-identical. NO wire change (gamma already rides KIN-002 / the projectile block). Per-airframe (initial balance, ~180-275 m): p47d 270, p51 275, spitfire 230, ki61 230, a6m2 250, la7 200, bf109 180, yak3 180. See ADR-Step7-Guns-Convergence.
weapons.attribution: Attacker attribution / 'who fired the killing round' (Step 7 guns, v1.16r0): each aircraft carries last_hit_by — the index of the aircraft whose round most recently damaged it, or -1 (NO_ATTACKER) if it has never been hit. It is set at hit time in the kernel from the striking round's owner (the round already carries owner for the G2 hit test), so it is the KERNEL-SIDE EVENT HOOK the earlier guns/netcode layers deferred (layer 6 could DERIVE hit/kill moments from hp deltas but could not say WHO). It persists through death, so at hp<=0 it names the killer -> an attributed kill-feed ('P-47D downed A6M2'). last_hit_by is the 11th per-aircraft snapshot f64 (appended after ammo) -> all 10 goldens move (last_hit_by = -1 identical for every aircraft that is never hit; GOLDEN-SK-Hit-001's a6m2 now records last_hit_by = 0, the p47d), exactly as gamma/hp/fire_cd/ammo each grew the snapshot. Proven additive: stripping the 11th f64 from every v1.16r0 golden reproduces its v1.15r0 hash byte-for-byte (attribution perturbs no trajectory). STILL no new det_math (a pure integer-valued state, like fire_cd/ammo). NOT yet on the WEAPON-001 wire (deferred one seal, exactly as ammo was kernel-state at G4 then rode the wire at v1.14r0); the wire transport + the layer-6 Event.attacker attributed kill-feed are the v1.17r0 follow-up. See ADR-Step7-Guns-Attribution-v1.16r0.
weapons.fire_input: Command.fire (per-tick gun trigger); the kernel gates it by the firer's per-airframe fire-rate cooldown (G3, v1.11r0) AND a non-empty magazine (G4, v1.13r0). A spawned round is boresight-zeroed to the firer's per-airframe convergence range (v1.15r0). A round that hits records its owner as the target's last_hit_by (attacker attribution, v1.16r0).
